summary: The W004/WIN-025 identity mapping is VERIFIED. results.json W004 title is literally "2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication" (confirmed via GitHub raw fetch of api/results.json). The two-scoreboards argument is valid. However, the parenthetical disclaimer in section text incorrectly says wins-page WIN-004 refers to "Noise Floor Limit" — it actually refers to "SAA — Dual-Lobe Split (Bottom Plate Leak)" per the dome's current predictions page.

results_json_w004.title: 2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication
results_json_w004.observed: Mixed (CMO: -17.6nT, NEW: -17.1nT. Others lost to noise)

wins_page_win004.title: SAA — Dual-Lobe Split (Bottom Plate Leak)
wins_page_win004.description: The SAA has split into two lobes — the primary Leaking Valve of the sub-terrestrial return path.
wins_page_win004.note: This is NOT Noise Floor Limit as our section text currently states

evidence.namespace_analysis: The dome uses two separate numbering systems: (1) W-numbers in results.json are monitoring API test IDs (W001=Lunar Transit, W004=Eclipse 9-Station, TASK-3-1=CHAOS-7 SAA, etc.), (2) WIN-numbers on the predictions/wins page are the public prediction scorecard (WIN-004=SAA Dual-Lobe Split). These are completely different indices with different orderings.

conclusion: The core two-scoreboards argument in SEC-2b.7 is VALID. W004 in results.json IS the 2024 eclipse 9-station test. The only factual error is the parenthetical: "not to be confused with wins-page WIN-004 which refers to Noise Floor Limit" should read "not to be confused with wins-page WIN-004 which refers to SAA — Dual-Lobe Split (Bottom Plate Leak)."

recommended_patch.old_text: not to be confused with wins-page WIN-004 which refers to Noise Floor Limit
recommended_patch.new_text: not to be confused with wins-page WIN-004 which refers to SAA — Dual-Lobe Split
recommended_patch.rationale: Wins-page WIN-004 is SAA Dual-Lobe Split, not Noise Floor Limit. The dome has no WIN specifically titled Noise Floor Limit. The two W-number namespaces (API test IDs vs public WIN numbers) are correctly documented but the cross-reference was wrong.

severity_assessment: The factual error is in a parenthetical clarification, not in the core argument. The two-scoreboards argument (results.json scores the eclipse test as confirmed_strong while the wins tab marks it REMOVED) is fully verified and stands. Downgrade from critical to minor — the core claim is correct, only the disambiguation note needs a one-phrase fix.
